canonicalizationRule: Recursive object-key sort, ascending lexicographic. Arrays preserve insertion order (semantically ordered: IO sequence and IOF sequence are content). Serialized via JSON.stringify(value, null, 2), UTF-8, LF line endings, trailing LF on file.

configurationSummary.$comment: Vendor-wide Configuration blob for the YourMembership Integration row. Targets the `upsert_integration_configuration` MCP tool documented in .claude/agents/metadata-writer.md. Each top-level key is named to match the workflow contract in that agent doc. NOTE: as of v5.39.x-phase0, the MJ: Integrations schema does NOT have a Configuration column — these facts are emitted here as a sidecar so the code-builder downstream has them, and so the runtime D4 SoftPKClassifier has the universalPK hint. When the Integration table gains a Configuration nvarchar(MAX) column (per plans/integration-framework-redesign.md), this file's contents copy verbatim into the Integration row's Configuration field.

APIVersioningStrategy.EvidenceFromOpenAPI: info.version='1.0, ServiceStack: 5.0.2.0' — only one published version; no /v1/, /v2/, no Accept-Version or X-Version header documented in any operation
APIVersioningStrategy.Note: No changelog or version history is published. The base path is `/` and resources sit directly under /Ams/{ClientID}/. ServiceStack runtime version (5.0.2) is exposed via the X-Powered-By response header but is the server framework, not the API contract version.

AdditionalNotes.BodyParamCasing: PascalCase in /Ams; snake_case in /OAuth/GetToken (RFC 6749 conformance); PascalCase in /OAuth/GetAccessToken (legacy YM shape)
AdditionalNotes.CORSAllowedHeaders: Content-Type, X-Requested-With, X-ss-id, Accept, Origin, Access-Control-Request-Method, Access-Control-Request-Headers, Authorization
AdditionalNotes.ServerVersion: ServiceStack/5.02 NET45 — visible in X-Powered-By header

AuthFlowDetail.ClientCredentialsEvidence: /OAuth/GetToken parameter list per OpenAPI: [grant_type, scope, code, refresh_token, redirect_uri, client_id, client_secret]. No client_credentials grant is documented or available; only authorization_code and refresh_token grants are present.

Legacy.Note: Legacy YM-specific shape (capitalised, AppSecret/AppSecert dual-spelling — vendor's typo deprecation banner). Prefer /OAuth/GetToken for new integrations.

Primary.SessionTTLSource: Getting Started with the REST API PDF (2021), §Authentication: 'Session expires after 15 minutes of inactivity'

AuthHeaderPattern.Note: CORS preflight reply at every endpoint lists both `X-ss-id` and `Authorization` in Access-Control-Allow-Headers. Either header is accepted depending on which auth flow issued the credential.

BulkOperationsAvailable.Note: These are bulk PUSH endpoints to a sibling Community Brands product (Informz). There is no general-purpose bulk read or batch import API for arbitrary YM resources. Other bulk-shaped endpoints: StoreProductBulkStatus, StoreProductBulkStatusAll, PeopleBulkDetach — each is resource-specific, not a generic batching surface.
BulkOperationsAvailable.Pattern: Informz bulk-upload family (cross-product, Informz email-marketing sibling product)

ClientIDHandling.Note: ClientID is REQUIRED in essentially every /Ams path. Connector code must inject it from the credential at request-build time. The integer Client ID is also echoed in every response's ResponseStatus envelope, so it round-trips.
ClientIDHandling.Source: Site-level identifier issued by YM admin console

ContentType.Note: From OpenAPI top-level. Note that auth endpoints accept formData (application/x-www-form-urlencoded) for session and OAuth flows — this is form-encoded post, not JSON. The /Ams data CRUD surface is JSON-only.

CustomFieldMarkerPattern.Note: YM exposes per-member custom fields via a separate CustomField endpoint family rather than inlining custom fields in the MemberProfile response. The CustomField response uses FieldCode (vendor-assigned) plus Values/ValuesProxy for the field's value list. There is NO marker pattern in field names (no `cf_`, no `custom_`) — the discriminant is the endpoint itself.
CustomFieldMarkerPattern.ResponseShape: CustomFieldResponse { FieldCode, Fields, Visibility, VisibilityInt, Values, ValuesProxy, MetaValue, ClientID }

ErrorResponseShape.ErrorCatalog: Vendor does not publish a closed list of ErrorCode values. Observed values include NotNull, Predicate. The catalog must be built empirically — recorded as a gap.
ErrorResponseShape.Note: Captured live from POST /Ams/Authenticate with empty body. ResponseStatus is present on EVERY response — success responses carry an empty ResponseStatus (no ErrorCode), error responses carry a populated one. Connector error-detection should test `body.ResponseStatus?.ErrorCode != null` rather than HTTP status code, because some validation errors return 200 with a populated ResponseStatus.

IncrementalSyncCapability.Note: No vendor-wide incremental-sync mechanism (no /sync endpoint, no Last-Modified-Since query semantic across all resources). Per-resource incremental support, where present, is via per-operation modifiedSince-style query parameters declared on individual paths. Therefore the vendor-level capability is FALSE; per-IO IncrementalSync flags live on MJ: Integration Objects.

PaginationDefaults.Note: 112 of 297 paths declare both PageSize and PageNumber query parameters. A minority of paths also accept OffSet/Offset/limit (5 distinct pagination parameter names observed across the spec) — per-operation pagination is captured in MJ: Integration Objects.PaginationType. The default for IOs that declare both PageSize+PageNumber is PageNumber pagination, 1-indexed. Default page size is per-operation (no global default published).

RateLimitPolicy.Evidence: Live HEAD requests to ws.yourmembership.com return no X-RateLimit-* headers, no Retry-After, no quota headers. SOURCE_STUDY §5 confirms vendor publishes no rate-limit documentation. Cloudflare front may apply edge-level protection but does not surface predictable quota info.
RateLimitPolicy.RecommendedClientBehaviour: Honour 429 responses if/when observed; back off on 503; no proactive throttling required by documentation but vendor-side throttling is plausible at high volume.

ResponseEnvelopeShape.ListPayloadKeyConvention: Per-operation key matches the resource (e.g., CampaignEmailLists, Rules, ConnectionCategories, EventRegistrantsList, GroupTypeList). The ResponseDataKey is per-IO, not vendor-wide.
ResponseEnvelopeShape.Note: Every /Ams response carries a ServiceStack envelope. Confirmed by inspection of multiple definitions (CampaignEmailListsResponse, DuesRulesResponse, ConnectionCategoryListResponse, EventRegistrantsResponse, MemberGroupsResponse).

RoleGating.Note: Per-operation `Requires any of the roles:` declarations on /metadata pages indicate role-gated operations. Connector callers must use credentials provisioned with the correct role for each operation. Per-IO role requirements should be captured on MJ: Integration Objects when known.

OAuth2.Note: Refresh-token expiry not documented. Initial token issuance happens via grant_type=authorization_code after the user is redirected through Admin → API Configuration → OAuth Management → Add Client App's configured Client App.

Session.Note: Session cookies do NOT have a refresh mechanism — when the SessionId expires, the client must call /Ams/Authenticate again with stored credentials.

WebhooksAvailable.Note: No first-party webhook documentation exists for YM. The OpenAPI surface includes ZoomEventListener and ZoomEventListenerOAuth endpoints (Zoom integration callbacks YM consumes from Zoom), but those are inbound from a third party into YM, not outbound YM-to-customer webhooks. SOURCE_STUDY §5 explicitly records this as a gap. No signature algorithm is documented.

configurationSummary.universalPKNote: YourMembership has NO vendor-wide PK convention. Different resource families use different PK shapes — ProfileID (int) for Members, MemberID + ClientID composite paths for Member sub-resources, EventID for Events, and per-resource integer IDs elsewhere. The runtime SoftPKClassifier must per-IO classify; there is no single fieldName that holds for all 160 Ams families. Source: walking the OpenAPI spec's path templates for `/Ams/{ClientID}/Member/{MemberID}/...` vs `/Ams/{ClientID}/Event/{EventId}/...` vs `/Ams/{ClientID}/CampaignEmailLists` parameter sets confirms the PK heterogeneity. Recorded explicitly so downstream agents do not invent a default.

fkTargetsMissing.details: Two distinct patterns observed: (1) 11 AmsEvent_* IOs reference target IO name "AmsEvent" — the AmsEvent base IO is absent from the contract while its 11 sub-IOs (Alias, AttendeeTypeSessions, ...) are present; (2) 7 IOs (Pinpoint, LocationCoordinates, JobAlertsCriteria, JobAlerts, JobSearch, SavedJobs, Templates) reference target name "YourMembership" which is the Integration row Name, not an IO. These broken refs were emitted upstream by ioiof-extractor and were NOT corrected by metadata-writer (whose scope is the Integration row + Configuration sidecar, not the IO graph). Documented here so downstream reviewer/code-builder agents see them at freeze-read time.
fkTargetsMissing.summary: 18 of 18 RelatedIntegrationObjectID references point to IO names that do not exist as IOs in this contract.

provenanceCoverage.summary: All 9 root Integration slots have provenance entries.

typeMismatches.summary: Zero type mismatches. IntegrationObjectField.Type column is nvarchar(100) free-form per MJIntegrationObjectFieldSchema in entity_subclasses.ts — no enum constraint to violate. All 2576 Type values are <=100 chars.

entries.value: YourMembership (Community Brands AMS) REST integration. Base URL https://ws.yourmembership.com, ServiceStack 5.0.2 backend. Supports two auth flows...

entries.rationale: ClassName is an integration-row identifier slot used by ConnectorFactory; it must match a registered @RegisterClass key. Retaining the existing value avoids a re-registration cascade. Not a hard-constraint vendor-fact; safely InferredFromContext.

sources.quote: Existing YM integration record uses ClassName='YourMembershipConnector' — value retained for compatibility with downstream consumers.

entries.rationale: MJ-internal packaging fact, not a vendor fact. The value is the canonical home for shipped connectors in this repo.

sources.quote: Existing YM record sets ImportPath='@memberjunction/integration-connectors'
host: 'ws.yourmembership.com', schemes: ['https'], basePath: '/'
Base URL https://ws.yourmembership.com per the 'Base URL' section

entries.rationale: Vendor publishes no documented per-app request quota. The MJ convention for 'no documented limit' is -1 (same as FileFeed). Recorded as ExplicitStatement-of-absence: I checked all four authoritative tier-1 sources and the live API headers, and none document a quota.

sources.quote: Spec contains no x-rate-limit-* extensions; no batch endpoint declared at vendor level (Informz bulk endpoints are resource-specific).
Cloudflare + ServiceStack response headers carry no X-RateLimit-*, no Retry-After, no quota indicator. Probed 2026-05-30 18:36 UTC.
PDF documents auth flow, pagination, and resource CRUD shape — does NOT publish a rate-limit policy.

entries.rationale: Mirrors BatchMaxRequestCount=-1 — no documented throttle window means -1 (disabled). Adopting the same -1/-1 pair as FileFeed.

sources.quote: No throttling or batch-window documentation in the spec.
No Retry-After, no documented backoff policy.

entries.rationale: Existing credential type matches the auth shape this connector requires (clientId, apiKey, apiPassword for session auth). OAuth Client App credentials would need a separate credential type if the OAuth flow is implemented; that is out-of-scope for the metadata row here and is captured in Configuration.AuthFlow.

sources.quote: Credential type 'YourMembership API' already exists with FieldSchema referencing clientId+apiKey+apiPassword.

entries.rationale: Cosmetic field; reuse of existing value avoids icon-shift in the UI.

sources.quote: Existing YM record uses fa-solid fa-id-card; AMS = member-card icon convention.

entries.rationale: YM has no vendor-wide PK convention — explicit null is the correct value, not omission. Runtime D4 SoftPKClassifier should classify per-IO. Recording explicit null prevents downstream agents from inventing a default.

sources.quote: Path templates vary across families: /Ams/{ClientID}/Member/{MemberID}/... uses MemberID; /Ams/{ClientID}/Event/{EventId}/... uses EventId; CampaignEmailLists uses ListId; MemberProfile uses ProfileID (int). No single PK field name holds for all 160 Ams resource families.

entries.rationale: Both flows are first-class. Session is the primary path (per the REST API PDF); OAuth2 authcode + refresh-token is the standards-conformant alternative (per OAuth PDF and OpenAPI). client_credentials is explicitly absent from the parameter list.

sources.quote: POST /Ams/Authenticate with ClientID/UserType/Username/Password returns SessionId used as x-ss-id header on subsequent calls; session expires after 15 minutes
OAuth flow with /OAuth/GetAccessToken parameters AppId+AppSecret+GrantType (values 'Code' or 'RefreshToken')
/OAuth/GetToken parameter list: [grant_type, scope, code, refresh_token, redirect_uri, client_id, client_secret]. No client_credentials grant declared.
Use SessionId as x-ss-id HTTP header

Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Content-Type, X-Requested-With, X-ss-id, Accept, Origin, Access-Control-Request-Method, Access-Control-Request-Headers, Authorization — both X-ss-id and Authorization headers are first-class
Pagination example: ?PageSize=5&PageNumber=1 with ListCount returned for the total.
112 of 297 paths declare both PageSize and PageNumber as parameters; ListCount appears in 656 schema definitions as the response total field
No /sync endpoint, no global Last-Modified-Since query semantic. Per-resource modifiedSince-style params exist on a minority of paths.
PDF does not describe any vendor-wide incremental sync mechanism
§5 Gaps: 'Webhook / push event subscription: ZoomEventListener paths exist in the OpenAPI but there is no narrative documentation of YM's own webhook model.'
ZoomEventListener / ZoomEventListenerOAuth paths exist but their schema is OAuth-callback shape from Zoom INTO YM, not outbound YM-to-customer webhooks.

InformzBulkUploadBySearchGuid, InformzBulkUploadEventRegistrants, InformzBulkUploadForDues, InformzBulkUploadForDuesBySearchId, InformzBulkUploadForOrders, InformzBulkUploadForOrdersBySearchId, InformzBulkUploadForReports — 7 paths with Bulk in the name; plus StoreProductBulkStatus and PeopleBulkDetach (per-resource)

entries.value: { Vendor: true (Informz family only); pattern: per-resource not generic }

entries.rationale: Explicit null: YM does not allow customers to define new top-level object types via the API.

sources.quote: No custom-object discovery endpoint. The 160 Ams resource families are all vendor-defined; customer-extensibility surfaces are limited to CustomField (per-member custom field values) and CustomForms/CustomPages (content). There is NO customer-creatable arbitrary object type with a marker pattern such as HubSpot's p_<accountID>_<name>.
definitions.CustomFieldResponse.properties = [FieldCode, Fields, Visibility, VisibilityInt, Values, ValuesProxy, MetaValue, ClientID]
§3.1 — CustomField is a discrete endpoint family separate from member profile data

entries.value: { Pattern: 'CustomField endpoint family'; Endpoints: ['/Ams/{ClientID}/Member/{MemberID}/CustomField', '/Ams/{ClientID}/CustomField']; field names carry NO marker prefix — discriminant is the endpoint }
